response.justification: This candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented generalist with a strong testing culture and genuine low-latency systems exposure, but he misses the role's hard minimum of 8 years of C++ — his professional C/C++ work totals roughly a year (National Instruments plus a thesis project) — and his large-scale infrastructure experience (~2 years of high-throughput microservices in DLang, some Kubernetes and AWS CDK) falls short of the 5-year bar. His leadership record is informal mentoring, workshops, and courses rather than owning technical strategy for a compute platform, which is the core of this Technical Lead position.
strengths: Experience with high-throughput, low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored test frameworks
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ — only about one year of professional C/C++ work
No formal technical-leadership role; mentoring has been informal rather than driving cross-team strategy
Cloud and distributed-infrastructure depth (~2 years) is well short of the 5-year requirement, and recent work is full-stack product engineering

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud infrastructure and technical-leadership track record
Strong engineering fundamentals and mentoring aptitude
